 {"id":"bd-podx","title":"Generate spec-to-test traceability matrix","description":"## Overview

Implement automated traceability from specification sections to conformance tests.

## Requirements

### Traceability Attribute
```rust
/// Mark a test with the spec section it validates.
#[proc_macro_attribute]
pub fn conformance(spec: &str, requirement: &str) -> TokenStream {
    // Generates test with metadata
}

// Usage:
#[conformance(spec = "3.2.1", requirement = "Region close waits for all children")]
#[test]
fn test_region_close_waits() {
    // ...
}
```

### Matrix Generation
```bash
asupersync conformance matrix

Output:
┌─────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────┐
│ Section │ Requirement                               │ Test              │
├─────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────┤
│ 3.2.1   │ Region close waits for all children       │ region_close_*    │
│ 3.2.2   │ Orphan tasks are prevented                │ no_orphan_tasks   │
│ 3.3.1   │ Cancel propagates to descendants          │ cancel_propagates │
│ 3.3.2   │ Drain phase allows cleanup                │ drain_phase_*     │
│ ...     │ ...                                       │ ...               │
└─────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────┘

Coverage: 45/50 requirements (90%)
Missing: 3.4.5, 3.6.2, 3.6.3, 3.7.1, 3.8.2
```

### Coverage Tracking
```rust
pub struct TraceabilityMatrix {
    pub entries: Vec<TraceabilityEntry>,
}

pub struct TraceabilityEntry {
    pub spec_section: String,
    pub requirement: String,
    pub test_name: String,
    pub test_file: PathBuf,
    pub test_line: u32,
}

impl TraceabilityMatrix {
    pub fn from_tests(tests: &[TestInfo]) -> Self;
    pub fn coverage(&self) -> f64;
    pub fn missing(&self) -> Vec<String>;
    pub fn to_markdown(&self) -> String;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
1. #[conformance] attribute macro
2. Matrix generation from test metadata
3. Coverage percentage calculation
4. Missing requirements report
5. Markdown export for documentation
6. CI integration (fail if coverage drops)

## Test Requirements
- Test macro generates correct metadata
- Test matrix generation finds all tagged tests
- Test coverage calculation
- Test markdown output format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueValley","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:03:40.122377138Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T06:18:14.771295294Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T06:18:14.771206368Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-pojj","title":"[EPIC-INFRA] OpenTelemetry Metrics Export","description":"## Overview

Implement OpenTelemetry metrics export for runtime observability, enabling integration with standard monitoring systems like Prometheus, Datadog, and Grafana.

## Strategic Value

**Problem Solved**: Production systems need metrics for monitoring and alerting. Without standard metrics export, operators must build custom solutions.

**Why OpenTelemetry**: OTel is the emerging standard for observability. It supports multiple backends (Prometheus, OTLP, etc.) through a single API.

**Production Requirement**: Any runtime targeting production use needs metrics. This is table stakes for enterprise adoption.

## Metrics Categories

### Runtime Metrics
- `asupersync.tasks.active` - Currently running tasks (gauge)
- `asupersync.tasks.spawned` - Total tasks spawned (counter)
- `asupersync.tasks.completed` - Tasks completed by outcome (counter, labeled)
- `asupersync.regions.active` - Currently open regions (gauge)
- `asupersync.regions.created` - Total regions created (counter)

### Timing Metrics
- `asupersync.task.duration` - Task execution time (histogram)
- `asupersync.region.lifetime` - Region open duration (histogram)
- `asupersync.scheduler.poll_time` - Time spent in poll (histogram)

### Cancellation Metrics
- `asupersync.cancellations` - Cancellation count by reason (counter, labeled)
- `asupersync.drain_duration` - Time spent in drain phase (histogram)

### Budget Metrics
- `asupersync.deadline.remaining` - Time remaining at task start (histogram)
- `asupersync.poll_quota.usage` - Poll quota utilization (histogram)

### Resource Metrics
- `asupersync.obligations.active` - Undischarged obligations (gauge)
- `asupersync.obligations.undischarged` - Obligations dropped undischarged (counter)

## Integration Design

### Feature Flag
```toml
[features]
metrics = ["opentelemetry", "opentelemetry-prometheus"]
```

### Metrics Provider
```rust
pub trait MetricsProvider: Send + Sync {
    fn task_spawned(&self, region_id: RegionId);
    fn task_completed(&self, task_id: TaskId, outcome: &Outcome<()>, duration: Duration);
    fn region_created(&self, region_id: RegionId, parent: Option<RegionId>);
    fn region_closed(&self, region_id: RegionId, lifetime: Duration);
    fn cancellation(&self, region_id: RegionId, kind: CancelKind);
    // ... etc
}
```

### OpenTelemetry Implementation
```rust
pub struct OtelMetrics {
    tasks_active: ObservableGauge<u64>,
    tasks_spawned: Counter<u64>,
    task_duration: Histogram<f64>,
    // ...
}

impl MetricsProvider for OtelMetrics { ... }
```

### Integration
```rust
RuntimeBuilder::new()
    .metrics(OtelMetrics::new(meter_provider))
    .build()
```

## Prometheus Example

```
# HELP asupersync_tasks_active Currently running tasks
# TYPE asupersync_tasks_active gauge
asupersync_tasks_active 42

# HELP asupersync_tasks_completed_total Tasks completed
# TYPE asupersync_tasks_completed_total counter
asupersync_tasks_completed_total{outcome="ok"} 1000
asupersync_tasks_completed_total{outcome="err"} 50
asupersync_tasks_completed_total{outcome="cancelled"} 10
asupersync_tasks_completed_total{outcome="panicked"} 2

# HELP asupersync_task_duration_seconds Task execution duration
# TYPE asupersync_task_duration_seconds histogram
asupersync_task_duration_seconds_bucket{le="0.001"} 500
asupersync_task_duration_seconds_bucket{le="0.01"} 800
...
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. MetricsProvider trait defined
2. OtelMetrics implementation
3. All metrics categories implemented
4. Feature-gated (no mandatory OTel dependency)
5. Prometheus exporter example
6. Grafana dashboard template
7. Documentation with setup guide

## Dependencies

- Optional opentelemetry dependency
- Uses runtime hooks for metrics collection

## Priority Rationale

Ranked #15 because while essential for production, metrics are a "nice to have" during development. Users can add metrics later once they move toward production.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"PinkMountain","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:14:44.596928289Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:23:58.140870651Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:23:58.140800200Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pojj","depends_on_id":"bd-ah3v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:59Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-pojj","depends_on_id":"bd-pga9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:59Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-pojj","depends_on_id":"bd-t613","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:59Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ptbf","title":"[Runtime] Preserve CancelReason across cancellation states","description":"Task cancellation transitions currently drop the CancelReason when moving from CancelRequested -> Cancelling/Finalizing, so TaskRecord::cancel_reason() cannot reflect the active reason across the full cancellation protocol (doc comment is inconsistent). Preserve the CancelReason in TaskState::Cancelling and TaskState::Finalizing, update request_cancel strengthening, and update lab oracles/tests accordingly. Acceptance: TaskState retains reason through cancellation protocol; TaskRecord::cancel_reason returns Some for CancelRequested/Cancelling/Finalizing; all tests and lab oracles compile+pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:36:51.651648365Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:43:26.435084545Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:43:26.435084545Z","close_reason":"Fixed: CancelReason is now preserved across Cancelling and Finalizing states. Tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-pu3k","title":"Implement single-level timer wheel","description":"## Overview

Implement a basic single-level timer wheel as the foundation for the hierarchical version.

## Background

Start simple with a single wheel, then extend to hierarchical. This allows validating the core mechanics before adding complexity.

## Design

### Wheel Structure
```rust
pub struct TimerWheel<const SLOTS: usize> {
    /// Circular array of timer slots
    slots: [TimerSlot; SLOTS],
    /// Current position in the wheel
    current: usize,
    /// Resolution in ticks (e.g., 1ms)
    resolution: Duration,
    /// Timers pending in the wheel
    count: usize,
}

struct TimerSlot {
    /// Head of intrusive linked list
    head: Option<NonNull<TimerNode>>,
}
```

### Timer Node (Intrusive)
```rust
pub struct TimerNode {
    /// Links for intrusive list
    next: Option<NonNull<TimerNode>>,
    prev: Option<NonNull<TimerNode>>,
    /// Waker to call on expiration
    waker: Option<Waker>,
    /// Slot this timer is in (for O(1) cancel)
    slot: usize,
    /// Absolute expiration time
    deadline: Instant,
}
```

### Operations
```rust
impl<const SLOTS: usize> TimerWheel<SLOTS> {
    pub fn insert(&mut self, node: &mut TimerNode, deadline: Instant);
    pub fn cancel(&mut self, node: &mut TimerNode);
    pub fn tick(&mut self) -> Vec<Waker>;  // Returns expired wakers
    pub fn next_expiration(&self) -> Option<Duration>;
}
```

## Implementation Notes

- Use intrusive lists to avoid allocation
- TimerNode is pinned (cannot move while in wheel)
- Slot index = (deadline / resolution) % SLOTS
- Tick advances current, processes slot

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single wheel with configurable slot count
- [ ] O(1) insert via slot hashing
- [ ] O(1) cancel via list removal
- [ ] O(1) tick processing (amortized)
- [ ] Unit tests for basic operations
- [ ] Handles wrap-around correctly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyOtter","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:02:39.751998165Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-20T23:54:09.966499884Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T23:54:09.965587526Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-q0om","title":"[Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8","description":"# [Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8

## Purpose
Migrate all unique sub-EPICs from asupersync-90l9 into asupersync-ds8 hierarchy, then close 90l9.

## Sub-EPICs to Migrate
- wx8h: Core Reactor
- l92b: epoll
- 3z8z: kqueue  
- 7tk3: Lab Reactor
- ui2r: TCP/UDP
- ycir: UDS
- 56fs: Integration Testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 90l9 sub-EPICs linked to ds8 hierarchy via dependencies
- [ ] 90l9 closed with migration note referencing ds8
- [ ] No orphaned sub-EPICs after merge
- [ ] ds8 description updated to reflect absorbed scope
- [ ] `br show asupersync-ds8` shows correct dependent tree

## Verification
```bash
br show asupersync-90l9  # Should be closed
br show asupersync-ds8   # Should show all I/O sub-EPICs
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'  # No cycles
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:16.599858279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548968635Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548896109Z","close_reason":"Verified: 90l9 already merged into ds8 hierarchy. All sub-EPICs (wx8h, l92b, 3z8z, 7tk3, ui2r, ycir) are closed. Remaining work (56fs, 5w2z, 8jx5) migrated to ds8.4 and ds8.3.
